title: FDA Panel Backs New mRNA Flu Vaccine After Recent Controversy
subtitle: Experts vote unanimously to support a new shot that could change how we fight seasonal influenza.

summary.en: An independent advisory panel for the FDA has voted to support Moderna’s new mRNA flu vaccine. The decision follows a period of disagreement within the agency regarding the review process. Data shows the vaccine is more effective than standard shots for adults over 50. Experts believe the mRNA platform will help the country respond faster to future virus strains. The FDA will now make a final decision on whether to approve the product for public use.
summary.zh: 一個獨立的 FDA 諮詢小組已投票支持莫德納的新型 mRNA 流感疫苗。此決定是在該機構內部針對審查程序出現分歧後所做出的。數據顯示，該疫苗對 50 歲以上成年人的效力優於標準疫苗。專家認為，mRNA 平台將有助於國家更快速地應對未來的病毒株。FDA 接下來將針對是否核准該產品供公眾使用做出最終決定。

paragraphs.en: A group of independent health experts has voted to support a new flu vaccine created by Moderna. On Thursday, the Vaccines and Related Biological Products Advisory Committee (VRBPAC) held an all-day meeting to review the vaccine, which is known as mFlusiva. The committee voted 9–0 to endorse the vaccine, stating that its benefits for adults aged 50 and older are greater than its potential risks.
paragraphs.zh: 一群獨立健康專家投票支持莫德納（Moderna）研發的新型流感疫苗。週四，疫苗與相關生物製品諮詢委員會（VRBPAC）召開了一整天的會議，審查這款名為 mFlusiva 的疫苗。委員會以 9 票對 0 票通過背書，指出該疫苗對 50 歲及以上成年人的益處大於潛在風險。

paragraphs.en: This vote is a significant step for the vaccine, which recently faced a difficult path to review. Earlier this year, the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) briefly refused to accept the application for the vaccine. According to STAT News, a top official at the agency initially blocked the review, claiming that the company had not compared its vaccine against the best available options for older adults. However, the agency later changed its position after Moderna provided more data and agreed to perform further studies after the vaccine is released to the public.
paragraphs.zh: 這次投票對該疫苗而言是重要的一步，因為它近期在審查過程中面臨重重困難。今年稍早，美國食品藥物管理局（FDA）曾一度拒絕受理該疫苗的申請。根據 STAT 新聞報導，該機構的一位高層官員起初阻撓了審查，聲稱該公司未將其疫苗與現有針對老年人的最佳選擇進行比較。然而，在莫德納提供更多數據並同意在疫苗上市後進行後續研究後，該機構隨後改變了立場。

paragraphs.en: The vaccine uses mRNA technology, the same method used to create highly effective COVID-19 shots. While this technology is well-known, it has recently faced criticism from some government leaders. MedPage Today reports that Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the current Secretary of Health and Human Services, has previously expressed concerns about mRNA technology and even cancelled federal funding for related research. Despite this, the panel of experts highlighted the clear advantages of using this platform for seasonal flu protection.
paragraphs.zh: 該疫苗採用信使核糖核酸（mRNA）技術，這與研發高效能新冠肺炎疫苗的方法相同。儘管這項技術廣為人知，但近期卻面臨部分政府領導人的批評。MedPage Today 報導指出，現任衛生與公共服務部部長小羅伯特·甘迺迪（Robert F. Kennedy Jr.）過去曾對 mRNA 技術表達擔憂，甚至取消了相關研究的聯邦資金。儘管如此，專家小組仍強調了使用此平台進行季節性流感防護的顯著優勢。

paragraphs.en: During the meeting, experts pointed to the vaccine’s strong performance in clinical trials. Data from a large study of over 40,000 adults aged 50 and older showed that the mRNA vaccine was about 27 percent more effective than standard flu shots. For those aged 65 and older, the results were even more promising. This group is particularly important because, according to CDC estimates cited by MedPage Today, people over 65 account for the majority of flu-related hospitalizations and deaths in the United States.
paragraphs.zh: 會議期間，專家們指出了該疫苗在臨床試驗中的強勁表現。一項針對超過 4 萬名 50 歲及以上成年人的大型研究數據顯示，mRNA 疫苗的效力比標準流感疫苗高出約 27%。對於 65 歲及以上的人群，結果更為樂觀。根據 MedPage Today 引述美國疾病管制與預防中心（CDC）的估計，這個族群尤為重要，因為 65 歲以上人口占了美國流感相關住院與死亡病例的大多數。

paragraphs.en: Flor Munoz-Rivas, a member of the advisory committee and a pediatric infectious disease expert at Baylor College of Medicine, praised the study results. She noted that the data was very clear and showed that the vaccine provides meaningful extra protection. She also highlighted that the mRNA platform allows scientists to develop vaccines much faster. This speed is essential for preparing for new, emerging strains of the flu or even future pandemics, she explained.
paragraphs.zh: 諮詢委員會成員、貝勒醫學院（Baylor College of Medicine）的小兒傳染病專家芙洛·穆諾茲-里瓦斯（Flor Munoz-Rivas）對研究結果表示讚賞。她指出數據非常明確，顯示該疫苗提供了顯著的額外保護。她也強調，mRNA 平台讓科學家能更快速地研發疫苗。她解釋說，這種速度對於應對新興流感病毒株或未來的流行病至關重要。

paragraphs.en: Other experts highlighted different benefits of the new technology. Hayley Gans, a doctor at Stanford Medicine Children's Health, noted that the vaccine appears to create a better T-cell response. T-cells are a vital part of the body’s immune system that help fight off infections. Gans explained that current flu vaccines often struggle to keep immunity levels high, but this new mRNA approach could offer longer-lasting protection.
paragraphs.zh: 其他專家則強調了這項新技術的其他益處。史丹佛醫學兒童健康中心（Stanford Medicine Children's Health）的醫師海莉·甘斯（Hayley Gans）指出，該疫苗似乎能產生更好的 T 細胞反應。T 細胞是人體免疫系統中對抗感染的重要部分。甘斯解釋說，目前的流感疫苗往往難以維持高水準的免疫力，但這種新的 mRNA 方法或許能提供更持久的保護。

paragraphs.en: Despite the positive vote, some members of the panel noted that the research had limitations. Adam Berger, a scientist from the National Institutes of Health, pointed out that the main study only looked at one flu season. He also noted that there is not yet enough data on how the vaccine works in people who are very frail or have weak immune systems. However, Berger concluded that the evidence was strong enough to suggest that the vaccine will be effective in the real world.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管投票結果正面，但部分委員會成員指出該研究存在局限性。美國國家衛生研究院（NIH）的科學家亞當·伯格（Adam Berger）指出，主要研究僅觀察了一個流感季節。他也提到，目前尚無足夠數據顯示該疫苗對身體極度虛弱或免疫系統較差的人群有何影響。不過，伯格總結認為，現有證據已足以證明該疫苗在現實世界中將會有效。

paragraphs.en: Moderna is now seeking two types of approval from the FDA. For adults aged 50 to 64, the company is asking for traditional approval. For those 65 and older, they are seeking an accelerated approval — a process that allows for faster access to medicines that treat serious conditions. To support this, Moderna has promised to conduct a large, multi-year study to compare the vaccine’s effectiveness in the real world.
paragraphs.zh: 莫德納目前正向 FDA 尋求兩種類型的核准。針對 50 至 64 歲的成年人，該公司申請的是傳統核准；對於 65 歲及以上的人群，他們則尋求加速核准，這是一種能讓治療嚴重疾病的藥物更快上市的程序。為了支持這項申請，莫德納承諾將進行一項大型、多年期的研究，以比較該疫苗在現實世界中的效力。

paragraphs.en: While the committee’s vote is not the final step, it is a major signal of support. The FDA usually follows the advice of its expert panels, though it is not required to do so. If the agency gives its final approval, it would mark a change in how the U.S. approaches annual flu prevention. As the country looks toward future flu seasons, this new technology could become a primary tool in protecting the health of older Americans.
paragraphs.zh: 雖然委員會的投票並非最終步驟，但這是一個強烈的支持訊號。FDA 通常會遵循其專家小組的建議，儘管並非強制要求。如果該機構給予最終核准，這將標誌著美國處理年度流感預防方式的轉變。隨著國家展望未來的流感季節，這項新技術可能成為保護美國老年人健康的主要工具。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop or prevent something from happening or moving forward.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（街區），這裡作動詞，指阻礙審查流程。文中：According to STAT News, a top official at the agency initially blocked the review, claiming that the company had not compared its vaccine against the best available options for older adults.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make up a particular amount or proportion of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的片語，容易被誤解為「解釋」。文中：This group is particularly important because, according to CDC estimates cited by MedPage Today, people over 65 account for the majority of flu-related hospitalizations and deaths in the United States.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To defend oneself against an illness or an attacker.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指免疫系統抵抗感染，而非字面上的「打架」。文中：T-cells are a vital part of the body’s immune system that help fight off infections.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be a sign of an important stage or event.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 標誌著、象徵（某個階段或事件）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（記號），這裡作動詞，指某事象徵著改變。文中：If the agency gives its final approval, it would mark a change in how the U.S. approaches annual flu prevention.

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: FDA Panel Endorses New Flu Shot That Uses Tech Dissed by RFK Jr.

sources.name: STAT News
sources.title: STAT+: FDA advisory panel endorses Moderna mRNA flu vaccine that was subject of controversy

previousArticles.title: New mRNA Flu Vaccine Faces Key Review by US Health Experts
previousArticles.summaryEn: US health advisers are meeting to discuss a new mRNA flu vaccine developed by Moderna. This vaccine is intended for people aged 50 and older to help reduce flu-related illnesses. While mRNA technology is known for its speed in production, the vaccine has faced some challenges during the approval process. The FDA has reviewed the data and found no major safety concerns so far. Experts will now decide if this new option is ready for the upcoming winter season.

quiz.question: What condition did the FDA set before ultimately agreeing to review Moderna's mFlusiva vaccine?

options.A: Moderna had to prove the vaccine was safer than COVID-19 mRNA shots.
options.B: Moderna had to provide additional data and commit to post-release studies.
options.C: Moderna had to lower the price of the vaccine for the elderly population.
options.D: Moderna had to secure funding from the Department of Health and Human Services.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，FDA 起初拒絕審查是因為認為 Moderna 未將疫苗與市面上最好的選項進行比較，後來在 Moderna 提供更多數據並同意在疫苗上市後進行進一步研究後，FDA 才改變立場。選項 A 在文中未提及；選項 C 關於價格的內容文中未提及；選項 D 錯誤，因為文中提到該部門負責人甚至取消了相關研究經費，並非提供經費。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might the FDA grant 'accelerated approval' specifically to the 65 and older age group?

options.A: Because this group has the highest rate of flu-related hospitalizations and deaths.
options.B: Because this group has already participated in the largest portion of clinical trials.
options.C: Because the mRNA technology is proven to be less effective in younger adults.
options.D: Because this group is less likely to experience side effects from the vaccine.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文章第四段提到，CDC 數據顯示 65 歲以上族群佔了美國流感相關住院與死亡的大多數，且第八段提到加速審核是用於治療嚴重疾病的藥物。結合兩者可推論，由於該族群風險最高，因此更需要加速取得疫苗以預防重症。選項 B 文中未提及誰參與最多；選項 C 文中提到該疫苗對 50 歲以上有效，並未說對年輕人無效；選項 D 文中未提及副作用與年齡的關聯。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'primary' most likely mean as used in the phrase 'a primary tool in protecting the health of older Americans'?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此語境中，primary 意指「主要的」、「首要的」，用來形容該疫苗技術將成為保護老年人健康的核心手段。選項 A (簡單的) 與文意不符；選項 C (時間順序上的第一) 雖然 primary 有此義，但在這裡是指重要性而非順序；選項 D (原始的) 在此處完全不適用。

quiz.question: What is the main message of this article?

options.A: Moderna's new mRNA flu vaccine has successfully replaced all traditional flu shots.
options.B: The FDA has officially approved mFlusiva for use by all American adults.
options.C: An expert panel has endorsed a new mRNA flu vaccine, which could significantly improve flu prevention for older adults.
options.D: The development of mRNA vaccines is currently being halted due to political opposition.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章核心在於報導 VRBPAC 專家委員會投票支持 Moderna 的 mFlusiva 疫苗，並討論了該疫苗對 50 歲以上族群的潛在優勢與科學依據。選項 A 錯誤，文中僅提到這是一種新技術，並未說已取代所有傳統疫苗；選項 B 錯誤，文中提到這還不是最終步驟，FDA 尚未給予最終核准；選項 D 錯誤，儘管有政治人物批評，但專家委員會仍給予了支持，研究並未停止。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
